feature_description: A Next.js 14 TypeScript application providing the visual scaffold for a pipeline run dashboard. It includes a fixed header with branding, tab-based view switching, and a call-budget progress bar; a History view showing a styled status table populated with hardcoded run records; a Run tab placeholder; and a Vitest smoke test verifying the History table renders correctly.

in_scope: Next.js 14 TypeScript project with JetBrains Mono (Google Fonts), body background #0a0b0e, and text color #cdd2db applied globally.

Fixed 50px header (background #101318, 1px bottom border #272d36) containing: a green logo dot, a bold CODEFORGE label, Run and History tab buttons that toggle the active view, and a call-budget progress bar displaying a hardcoded current/ceiling agent-call count.

History view (default active tab): a full-width scrollable table with column headers STATUS | FEATURE | RUN ID | REACHED | WHEN. Each row has a 2px left-border accent colored by its status, a status dot and uppercase label, the feature brief text, run ID, last phase reached, and a relative timestamp string. Exactly 7 hardcoded rows covering all four status states: succeeded (oklch(0.79 0.14 153)), running (oklch(0.76 0.13 187)), awaiting_human (oklch(0.82 0.14 119)), failed_terminal (oklch(0.66 0.21 27)).
Run tab view: a placeholder div rendered when the Run tab is active.
A Vitest smoke test that mounts the History view component and asserts exactly 7 data rows are present in the DOM.
explicitly_out_of_scope: Any real data fetching, API routes, or backend integration.
Persistent tab state (e.g. URL routing per tab).
Additional test coverage beyond the single smoke test.
Interactivity within table rows (e.g. click-to-expand, sorting, filtering).

acceptance_criteria.description: The page renders a fixed-position header exactly 50px tall with background #101318 and a 1px bottom border in #272d36. The header contains a green logo dot, a bold 'CODEFORGE' label, two tab buttons labelled 'Run' and 'History', and a call-budget progress bar that displays a current agent-call count against a ceiling (e.g. '12 / 50 calls') with a filled bar reflecting the ratio.

On initial load the History tab is active and the History view is visible. The view renders a scrollable table with exactly the five column headers STATUS, FEATURE, RUN ID, REACHED, and WHEN, and exactly 7 data rows. Each row carries a 2px left-border accent whose color matches its status value: succeeded → oklch(0.79 0.14 153), running → oklch(0.76 0.13 187), awaiting_human → oklch(0.82 0.14 119), failed_terminal → oklch(0.66 0.21 27). Each row also displays a status dot in the same accent color, an uppercase status label, a feature description string, a run ID string, a last-phase-reached string, and a relative timestamp string. All four status states appear at least once across the 7 rows.

Clicking the Run tab hides the History table and renders the Run placeholder div; clicking the History tab restores the History table and hides the Run placeholder. Only one view is visible at a time.

The Vitest smoke test imports and mounts the History view component in a jsdom environment and asserts that exactly 7 table data rows are present in the rendered output. The test suite passes with no errors.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the hardcoded row, used as React key.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Pipeline run status; drives row accent color and label.

fields.name: feature
fields.description: Short description of the feature the run was building.

fields.name: runId
fields.description: Human-readable run identifier string displayed in the RUN ID column.

fields.name: reached
fields.description: Name of the last pipeline phase reached (e.g. 'requirements', 'coder', 'reviewer').

fields.name: when
fields.description: Pre-formatted relative timestamp string (e.g. '2 hours ago') for display in the WHEN column.

fields.name: color
fields.description: oklch color string used for the row left-border accent and status dot.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Uppercase display label for the status.

relationships: StatusConfig entries are keyed by RunRecord.status to resolve display color and label for each row.
